title: Incommensurate Antiferromagnetic Order in the Fe-substituted Bi-2201 Cuprate in the Heavily Overdoped Regime
authors: Y. Komiyama, Y. Ikeda, T. Taniguchi, T. J. Williams, M. Matsuda, S. Asai, T. Masuda, H. Kuwahara, H. Kuroe, T. Kawamata, I. Watanabe, M. Fujita, T. Adachi
abstract: Elastic neutron scattering experiments showed incommensurate antiferromagnetic peaks in 5% Fe-substituted Bi-2201 cuprate in the non-superconducting heavily overdoped regime. The incommensurability delta~0.21 is comparable to that observed in Fe-substituted Bi-2201 in the overdoped regime. [Hiraka et al., Phys. Rev. B 81, 144501 (2010).] The magnetic correlation length is comparable between the overdoped and non-superconducting heavily overdoped regimes. It is plausible that incommensurate antiferromagnetic order is induced and stabilized by Fe in the heavily overdoped regime, which suggests a robust antiferromagnetic correlation beyond the superconducting dome in the phase diagram.
summary: This paper demonstrates that incommensurate antiferromagnetic order exists in the heavily overdoped, non-superconducting regime of Bi-2201 cuprates when stabilized by iron substitution. The findings suggest that robust antiferromagnetic correlations persist even beyond the superconducting dome. The observed magnetism appears to be a glassy state induced by the pinning of intrinsic fluctuations by Fe impurities.
main_problem: The study investigates whether incommensurate antiferromagnetic (AFM) order or fluctuations persist in the non-superconducting, heavily overdoped regime of Bi-2201 cuprates.
main_result: The researchers discovered incommensurate AFM peaks in 5% Fe-substituted Bi-2201 in the heavily overdoped regime, suggesting that Fe-substitution stabilizes existing AFM fluctuations into a glassy magnetic state.
method: Elastic neutron scattering experiments were performed using triple-axis spectrometers (TOPAN, HER, and HB-3) on single crystals, supplemented by magnetic susceptibility and muon spin rotation (muSR) measurements.
model_or_system: The physical system is Fe-substituted Bi-2201 cuprate (Bi1.74Pb0.38Sr1.88Cu1-yFeyO6+d) in the heavily overdoped, non-superconducting regime (hole concentration p approx 0.29 to 0.31).
key_observables: Incommensurability (delta), magnetic correlation length (xi_mag), magnetic transition temperature (Tm), and hole concentration (p).
important_parameters: Fe-substitution level (y = 0.05), hole concentration (p approx 0.29-0.31), and temperature.
main_assumptions: The study assumes that the observed magnetic order is induced/stabilized by Fe impurities acting on intrinsic AFM fluctuations.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows the difference profile of elastic neutron scattering and the scan trajectory; Figure 2 shows the temperature dependence of net counts; Figure 3 shows the hole-concentration dependence of incommensurability across various cuprates.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of magnetism in the heavily overdoped regime, describes the experimental setup and sample preparation, presents neutron scattering results showing incommensurate peaks, discusses the temperature dependence and glassy nature of the magnetism, and concludes with a discussion on the mechanism of Fe-induced stabilization.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

Inset shows the scan trajectory in the reciprocal (h, k) plane of the orthorhombic notation. Solid line is a ﬁtting result using Eq. (1).

Fig. 2. Solid lines represent the background, derived from the temperature- dependent intensity changes at the (h, k) position unaﬀected by the IC peaks, extrapolated from the data at the highest temperature. Error bars at the end of background lines represent errors in the intensity change. The open circle in (a) represents the background value at Q = (0.78,-0.22) in Fig. 1 added to the 50 K data.
